title: Personal IT Forecast Research Compendium (2000-2001)
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: A curated research compendium assembled by Peter S. Kastner capturing key PC and semiconductor market forecasts from Dataquest, IDC, Merrill Lynch, TechWeb, and Japanese industry sources during the 2000-2001 PC downturn. Covers worldwide PC shipment declines, U.S. market contraction, DRAM price collapse, Japan/Asia growth, and Kastner's own dissent [PSK disagrees] on Rambus DRAM demand expectations. Source material for Aberdeen's Personal IT practice forecasting work.
importance_rationale: Captures the primary-source forecast inputs (Dataquest, IDC, Merrill Lynch) Kastner used to launch Aberdeen's Personal IT practice in 2001; includes his own editorial dissent on Rambus demand.
relevance_rationale: Provides a contemporaneous record of how analysts framed the first-ever PC shipment decline -- directly relevant to studying analyst accuracy and tech market inflection points.
prescience_rationale: Embedded forecasts (e.g., Dataquest 2005 chip revenue of $299.85B) proved broadly accurate in direction; Rambus DRAM mainstream adoption did not occur, partially validating PSK's skepticism.
